creator: rz-rpi03@h-ka.de
text:
(In reply to Ed Maste from comment #6)
> This would be fine, it will leave existing devices (outside of the RPi world)
> working as they do now, and fix the onboard RPi3B+ Ethernet; [...]

After some research I have not found - or identified - how a/the Linux lan78xx
driver uses EEPROM data to enable the LEDs.
So I changed the patch to not touch the LEDs if there is no pattern information
avaliable.

Now muge_set_leds() has a condition if there is a EEPROM detected. Which does
nothing at the moment. This should handle the case with existing devices.

In the Raspberr Pi git there is a different apporach to set the LEDs from
branch rpi-4.14.y to rpi-4.17.y where the values (0..5,14,15) of the
"microchip,led-modes" array elements are used to set up the pattern.
Newer branches dropped this in favor of using the number of array elements,
like this patch does as well.

Since branch rpi-4.14.y LED0 and LED1 are enabled unconditionally if there is
no EEPROM and no FDT found.
